title: Krylov Complexity for Plane Wave Matrix Model
abstract: We study Krylov complexity in BMN Plane Wave Matrix Model at large mass deformation. We consider various consistent reductions of the matrix model that allow us to perform a Hamiltonian analysis which leads to different notions of the Krylov complexity. In the first part of the paper, we study the Krylov state complexity considering systematic reduction of $N=3$ and $N=4$ representations of the matrix model, which reveals a universal characteristic scaling for the Lanczos coefficients and fix them completely in terms of the mass deformation parameter. In the second part of the paper, we study the Krylov operator growth in the matrix model and compute the corresponding Lanczos coefficients. In both cases, we observe a \emph{linear} scaling of Lanczos coefficients with the mass parameter. The early time growth in Krylov complexity receives quadratic correction due to the presence of the massive deformation in the matrix model. Our analysis reveals that such massive corrections appear at same order in time for both the notion of the Krylov complexity.
summary: This paper explores how mass deformation influences the growth of complexity in the BMN matrix model. It demonstrates that both state and operator complexities share a universal quadratic time-dependence at early stages and that Lanczos coefficients scale linearly with the mass parameter.
main_problem: The paper investigates the growth of Krylov state complexity (KSC) and Krylov operator complexity (KOC) in the BMN Plane Wave Matrix Model, specifically focusing on how mass deformation affects complexity growth.
main_result: The study finds that Lanczos coefficients scale linearly with the mass deformation parameter (mu) and that early-time complexity growth exhibits a universal quadratic correction proportional to (mu^2 * t^2).
method: The authors use Hamiltonian analysis and the Lanczos algorithm on reduced matrix models (N=3, N=4) in the large mass deformation limit.
model_or_system: The BMN Plane Wave Matrix Model, which is a massive deformation of the BFSS matrix model describing membranes on an 11d pp-wave background, including reduced models like the Fuzzy Sphere and Coulomb potential models.
key_observables: Krylov state complexity (KSC), Krylov operator complexity (KOC), and Lanczos coefficients (a_n and b_n).
important_parameters: Mass deformation parameter (mu), Coulomb charge (Q), and the large mass deformation limit (mu -> infinity).
main_assumptions: The analysis is restricted to the bosonic sector, assumes the large mass deformation limit, and utilizes the Weyl gauge (A=0).
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the early-time growth of complexity for different models; Figure 2 illustrates the dependence of the complexity pre-factor (zeta) on the Coulomb charge (Q), showing a non-monotonic behavior.
paper_structure: The paper is structured into two main parts: the first analyzes Krylov state complexity using N=3 and N=4 reductions, and the second investigates Krylov operator growth, followed by a comparative analysis of scaling laws and early-time dynamics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. We plot early time growth in complexity for different values of the Coulmb charge Q.
Figure 2. Plot the LO coefficient ζ with the Coulomb charge Q.
